info.title: HRCore API
info.description: Initial OpenAPI-first contract seed for HRCore.

get.description: Returns the PoC-depth synthetic Okta mastering run evidence needed for operator inspection. This is not a raw payload viewer, CSV export surface, production audit archive, or WORM log.

get.summary: Inspect bounded MVP-A onboarding audit evidence by correlation ID
get.description: Returns the repo-owned MVP-A onboarding correlation trace summary for one explicit correlation ID after runtime actor, tenant/environment, field-scope, and data-scope checks plus request-owner binding. Requested scope headers and request-owner binding are validated before the full trace verifier returns correlation-specific evidence. Trace summary fields are filtered to the authorized evidence surfaces and field scopes. This bounded surface fails closed when required linked evidence is missing and omits later-wave disclosure, archive, provider-search, and production field-permission behavior.

200.description: Bounded MVP-A onboarding correlation trace evidence.

403.description: Runtime actor, tenant/environment, field-scope, or data-scope authorization failed closed.

409.description: Required linked onboarding audit evidence was missing or inconsistent.

503.description: The local MVP-A onboarding audit trace database was not configured.

post.summary: Record a bounded MVP-A onboarding support review
post.description: Creates a reasoned support review for one explicit MVP-A onboarding correlation ID. The support actor, tenant/environment, reason, evidence surfaces, and field scopes fail closed before trace evidence is returned. The operation records local audit/correlation evidence and keeps unredacted request-body disclosure, broad audit lookup, provider-side audit lookup, production WORM/Object Lock, hash-chain archive, compliance restore, and production support procedure readiness blocked.

201.description: Reasoned support review evidence was recorded.

403.description: Support actor, reason, tenant/environment, or requested evidence scope failed closed.

409.description: Required directly linked onboarding evidence was missing or inconsistent.

503.description: The local MVP-A onboarding support review database was not configured.

get.summary: Render MVP-A new hire onboarding wizard
get.description: Returns the MVP-A-only new hire wizard surface for draft, validation, and submit. Later-wave privacy, provider, and production control surfaces remain outside this operation.

post.summary: Validate an MVP-A onboarding transaction request
post.description: Validates the submitted body through the transaction_request contract without applying business rules in the UI layer.

200.description: The request is valid for the MVP-A onboarding contract.

400.description: The request violated the MVP-A onboarding transaction_request contract.

post.summary: Save or submit an MVP-A onboarding transaction request
post.description: Creates, edits draft, or submits an MVP-A onboarding transaction_request. Draft edits are accepted only for the same explicit request, person, and correlation binding.

200.description: An existing draft request was edited.

201.description: A new draft was created, a request was submitted, or an idempotent submit was accepted.

409.description: The request conflicts with an existing authoritative transaction_request binding.

503.description: The local onboarding transaction_request database was not configured.

post.summary: Record an MVP-A onboarding approval decision
post.description: Applies the MVP-A onboarding approval state machine for approve, return, reject, and cancel decisions. This records local audit evidence only; production RBAC, self-approval prevention, break-glass, two-key controls, and advanced delegation remain outside this child.

200.description: The approval decision was applied or an idempotent retry was recognized.

404.description: The onboarding transaction request decision target was not found.

409.description: The decision is illegal for the current authoritative transaction_request state.

503.description: The local onboarding transaction_request database was not configured.

post.summary: Apply an approved MVP-A onboarding transaction request
post.description: Commits the approved onboarding request into local HR Core person, employment, assignment, lifecycle, and audit evidence. Okta projection, provider writeback, later transfer, termination, leave, and regulated-data behavior remain outside this operation.

200.description: The approved onboarding request was applied or an idempotent completed retry was recognized.

409.description: The request is not approved or the completed apply evidence conflicts with the retry command.

503.description: The local onboarding transaction_request database was not configured.

get.description: Returns the MVP-B-only assignment transfer wizard surface for draft, validation, and submit. Raw payload disclosure, CSV/export, real-data upload, broad employee search, live-provider, and production control surfaces remain outside this operation.

post.summary: Validate an MVP-B transfer transaction request
post.description: Validates the submitted body through the bounded MVP-B transfer transaction_request contract without applying business rules in the UI layer.

200.description: The request is valid for the MVP-B transfer contract.

400.description: The request violated the MVP-B transfer transaction_request contract.

post.summary: Save or submit an MVP-B transfer transaction request
post.description: Creates or submits a bounded MVP-B transfer transaction_request for repo-owned synthetic data only. Unsupported later-wave fields, raw payload disclosure, CSV/export, real-data upload, broad employee search, and live-provider inputs fail outside this contract.

201.description: A transfer draft was created, a request was submitted, or an idempotent retry was accepted.

409.description: The request conflicts with an existing authoritative transfer transaction_request binding.

503.description: The local transfer transaction_request database was not configured.

get.description: Returns the MVP-C-only termination wizard surface for draft, validation, and submit. Raw payload disclosure, CSV/export, real-data upload, broad employee search, live-provider, retention/deletion job, anonymization, legal-hold, and production control surfaces remain outside this operation.

post.summary: Validate an MVP-C termination transaction request
post.description: Validates the submitted body through the bounded MVP-C termination transaction_request contract without applying business rules in the UI layer.

200.description: The request is valid for the MVP-C termination contract.

400.description: The request violated the MVP-C termination transaction_request contract.

post.summary: Save or submit an MVP-C termination transaction request
post.description: Creates or submits a bounded MVP-C termination transaction_request for repo-owned synthetic data only. Unsupported later-wave fields, raw payload disclosure, CSV/export, real-data upload, broad employee search, live-provider, retention/deletion job, anonymization, legal-hold, and production inputs fail outside this contract.

201.description: A termination draft was created, a request was submitted, or an idempotent retry was accepted.

409.description: The request conflicts with an existing authoritative termination transaction_request binding.

503.description: The local termination transaction_request database was not configured.

get.description: OpenAPI-first contract for a server-authorized, repository-owned synthetic employee collection. Actor context is resolved by the server; client persona state is not authoritative. The operation uses allowlisted filters and keyset pagination and does not expose raw payloads, sensitive personal information, broad search, unrestricted counts, real employee data, or production-ready authorization.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Employee ID or display-name prefix. Wildcard and regex expressions are not accepted.

parameters.name: organizationCode
parameters.description: Exact organization code within the server-resolved actor scope.

parameters.name: asOf
parameters.description: Effective date for employment and assignment joins. When omitted, the server resolves the initial request acceptance date in UTC, returns it in appliedFilters, and binds it to the cursor fingerprint.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque authenticated p2list_cursor_v1 random handle for 15-minute server-side continuation state bound to authorization context and canonical filters.

400.description: A filter, sort, date, limit, or cursor failed closed.

401.description: Authoritative server-side actor context is missing or invalid.

403.description: Permission or data-scope authorization denied the collection without revealing record existence.

get.summary: List bounded synthetic lifecycle transaction requests
get.description: OpenAPI-first contract for a server-authorized collection that normalizes onboarding, transfer, and termination requests. Type-specific payloads remain on existing detail surfaces. The operation does not expose raw payloads, free-form notes, broad history export, real employee data, or production-ready authorization.

parameters.name: subjectEmployeeId
parameters.description: Exact employment code for the subject.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Request ID, employee ID, or display-name prefix. Wildcard and regex expressions are not accepted.

parameters.name: organizationCode
parameters.description: Exact organization code within the server-resolved actor scope.

parameters.name: requestedBy
parameters.description: Exact requester actor ID when permitted.

parameters.name: decidedBy
parameters.description: Exact decision actor ID when permitted.

parameters.name: requestedFrom
parameters.description: Requested-at range start. Must be supplied with requestedTo.

parameters.name: requestedTo
parameters.description: Requested-at range end. Must be supplied with requestedFrom; the range is limited to 366 days.

parameters.name: effectiveFrom
parameters.description: Effective-date range start. Must be supplied with effectiveTo.

parameters.name: effectiveTo
parameters.description: Effective-date range end. Must be supplied with effectiveFrom; the range is limited to 366 days.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque authenticated p2list_cursor_v1 random handle for 15-minute server-side continuation state bound to authorization context and canonical filters.

400.description: A filter, sort, date range, limit, or cursor failed closed.

401.description: Authoritative server-side actor context is missing or invalid.

403.description: Permission or data-scope authorization denied the collection without revealing request existence.

post.summary: Export a bounded filtered synthetic employee list
post.description: Contract-only synchronous CSV export for a server-authorized, meaningfully filtered result of at most 100 synthetic rows. The server owns columns and rows, rejects rather than truncates, and does not provide broad, scheduled, long-lived, real-data, or production export.

400.description: The export request or reason was malformed.

401.description: Authoritative server-side actor context is missing or invalid.

403.description: Export permission, download permission, or data scope denied the request.

422.description: A meaningful filter was absent, the result exceeded 100 rows, or a prohibited export surface was requested.

post.summary: Export a bounded filtered synthetic lifecycle request list
post.description: Contract-only synchronous CSV export for a server-authorized, meaningfully filtered lifecycle result of at most 100 synthetic rows. The server owns columns and rows, rejects rather than truncates, and does not provide raw payload, broad history, scheduled, real-data, or production export.

400.description: The export request or reason was malformed.

401.description: Authoritative server-side actor context is missing or invalid.

403.description: Export permission, download permission, or data scope denied the request.

422.description: A meaningful filter was absent, the result exceeded 100 rows, or a prohibited export surface was requested.

post.summary: Ingest synthetic work email writeback evidence
post.description: Accepts local-only synthetic provider-confirmed work email writeback events. This is not a production webhook, signature verification surface, real Okta call, approval workflow, or audit immutability claim.

400.description: Synthetic writeback input was malformed or violated local synthetic constraints.

201.description: Synthetic writeback event was persisted and either applied or recorded as PoC conflict evidence.

503.description: The local writeback ingest database was not configured.

P2ListCursor.description: Opaque authenticated p2list_cursor_v1 random handle with at least 128 bits of entropy and a 15-minute expiry. The wire token carries only version, random stateId, and expiresAt; it contains neither PII nor raw search terms. Sort continuation values, including display names, remain in non-logged server-side state bound to the authorization context and canonical filter fingerprint. Employee state additionally carries resolvedAsOf.

requestedAt.description: RFC 3339 input normalized to a UTC instant and canonical YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.sssZ value before filtering, sorting, or cursor-state persistence. Text comparison is prohibited.
